revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:彦狭知命]]
{{Infobox deity | Name = 彦狭知命 (Hiko-Sachi-no-Mikoto) | Generation name = | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine Rank = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full name = 彦狭知命 | Other name = 彦狭知神 | Alternative names = | Divine status = Shield-making, {{ill|Woodworking|en|Woodworking|qid=Q816871|lt=Woodcraft|1=Woodworking}} | Mausoleum = | Father = | Mother = | Parent = | Siblings = | Consort = | Children = | Palace = | Shrines = {{ill|Nakoshi-yama Shrine|ja|莫越山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoshi-yama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578897|1=Nakoshi-yama Shrine}}, {{ill|Tatenui Shrine|ja|楯縫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatenui Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯縫神社 (美浦村郷中)|1=Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17211507}}, etc. | Related clans = {{ill|Kikoku-no-Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku-no-Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604953}}, Kii Inbe (紀伊忌部) }} '''Hiko-Sachi-no-Mikoto''' (彦狭知命) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview == In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', this deity is referred to as '''Hiko-Sachi-no-Kami''' (彦狭知神). Regarding the name, "Hiko" is interpreted as "a distinguished man," and "Sachi" as "iron arrow."<ref>''Nihon Shoki'' (Vol. 1), {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|qid=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten|1=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1994, pp. 141-142.</ref>
In the second volume of the ''Nihon Shoki'', ninth section, second alternative writing, Hiko-Sachi-no-Mikoto appears alongside his father, {{ill|Te-Oki-Hoi-no-Kami|ja|手置帆負神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Te-Oki-Hoi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578900|1=Te-Oki-Hoi-no-Kami}}, and is described as a shield-maker (盾縫い). Similarly, in the ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi|1=Kogo Shūi}}'', in the section about the divine age, he appears with his father, using {{ill|Ama-no-Mikahari|ja|天御量|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Mikahari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578903|1=Ama-no-Mikahari}} to cut trees in valleys and construct a sacred palace (瑞殿), as well as to produce sacred items like parasols, spears, and shields. In the same text's account of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Hiko-Sachi-no-Mikoto is mentioned again, collaborating with his father and following {{ill|Futodama|lt=Futotama-no-Mikoto|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}'s grandson, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, to fell trees and construct the emperor’s main hall. His descendants are said to reside in the two villages of Miki and Araka in {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}'s Nagusa District. The ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' ("Chronicles of the Emperors") conveys a similar account as found in the ''Kogo Shūi''.
== References == {{reflist}}
